raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased a camera and memory cards from XXXX with order number XXXX on XX/XX/year>. Unfortunately, I was dissatisfied with the camera and requested a return. XXXX accepted the return and provided a XXXX return label with tracking number XXXX. I shipped the product back via XXXX on XX/XX/year>. However, after a week, when I contacted XXXX to request a refund, I was informed that they had not yet received the package. They later confirmed that XXXX had lost the package. 
I reached out to XXXX, and they confirmed that the package was indeed lost. XXXX further advised that XXXX should file a claim to recover the value of the package, since they are the owner of the return label and I, as the customer, have no control over the shipment. XXXX subsequently filed a claim with XXXX, but now they are charging me the full amount for the items, citing that they have not received the package. 
Since I returned the product using the return label provided by XXXX, I should not be held responsible for the lost package. I paid for this order using my American Express card, and as a result, I filed a dispute regarding this transaction. However, American Express has sided with XXXX and closed the dispute, despite my providing all the necessary documentation, which includes : 1.Order invoice from XXXX ,2XXXX return label provided by XXXX ,3.XXXX tracking details ,4.Email from XXXX  acknowledging that they lost the package Given that I followed the correct return process and the package was lost by UPS, I believe this issue should be resolved fairly, and I should not be held liable for the charge. 
To escalate the issue, I contacted an American Express supervisor, XXXX XXXX, but he has repeatedly told me that he would call me back regarding the dispute and has not followed through on any of his promises to respond. I am extremely frustrated with this lack of follow-up and transparency, especially given that I have provided all the relevant documentation to support my case. Given that I followed the correct return process and the package was lost by XXXX, I believe this issue should be resolved fairly, and I should not be held liable for the charge.
